task_completion.description: Check if the required layers are created in LibreCAD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface). It should display the layers 'Court_Lines', 'Net_Line', and 'Annotations'.

task_completion.description: Check if the main outer rectangular boundary of the court is drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing area for a large white rectangle representing the outer boundary of the badminton court.

task_completion.description: Check if the internal court lines (singles sidelines, service lines, center line) are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the main rectangle for white lines: horizontal lines near the top and bottom edges (singles sidelines), vertical lines near the left and right edges (doubles service lines), vertical lines closer to the center (short service lines), and a horizontal center line that is broken in the middle area between the short service lines.

task_completion.description: Check if the net line is drawn correctly on its layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a vertical red dashed line exactly in the middle of the court. It should extend slightly above and below the outer boundary rectangle.

task_completion.description: Check if the required text annotations are added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for yellow text reading 'NET' near the center red line, and 'SERVICE LINE' near one of the vertical service lines.

task_completion.description: Check if the drawing is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the LibreCAD window or a save dialog. It should show 'badminton_court.dxf' indicating the file has been saved.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing interaction with LibreCAD's drawing tools, layer management, and command line interface at the bottom of the window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is interacting with LibreCAD.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application visible in the screenshots must be LibreCAD, not a text editor or a script running in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a script to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing Python scripts or echo commands being used to generate the DXF file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The badminton court dimensions are 13400x6100. The net line is at X=6700. Singles sidelines are at Y=460 and Y=5640. Short service lines are at X=4720 and X=8680. Long service lines are at X=760 and X=12640. Center line is at Y=3050. Required layers: 'Court_Lines' (White, Continuous), 'Net_Line' (Red, Dashed), 'Annotations' (Yellow, Continuous). The file must be saved as badminton_court.dxf.
